findings.description: The audit is grounded in the packet-level PETS/PlaNet facts but does not consistently label which claims are direct packet evidence versus proposed protocol requirements.
findings.evidence: It states PETS uses probabilistic dynamics ensembles with MPC and PlaNet learns latent dynamics from pixels, then recommends many baselines and diagnostics without explicit evidence labels.

findings.description: Some recommendations are slightly more specific than the packet supports, such as oracle/state variants, area under learning curve, and particular planner diagnostics, though they are presented as requirements rather than source facts.
findings.evidence: The revised protocol proposes state-based continuous-control and pixel-based DeepMind Control-style splits plus detailed metrics not supplied in the packet.

scorecards.summary: Strong RL-specific audit that captures learned model assumptions, planning risk, model-error accumulation, baselines, seeds, metrics, and claim boundaries. It is highly useful as a revised protocol, with only moderate traceability limitations.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. It does not invent numeric results or present broad superiority claims, but source grounding is mostly generic rather than location-specific.

findings.description: It is somewhat over-conservative in saying the packet only provides two source notes, while the task/gold indicates packet evidence locations include abstracts, model/planning sections, experiments, baselines, figures, and limitations, though exact details may be unavailable.
findings.evidence: Opening sentence: "It only provides two source notes"; frozen gold references PETS/PlaNet experiment sections, baseline tables/figures, environment descriptions, and evaluation protocol sections as evidence locations to verify.

findings.description: The revised protocol is actionable but less complete than stronger variants on planning compute, metric detail, and explicit separation of sample efficiency, policy performance, world-model fidelity, and planning success.
findings.evidence: It includes core requirements but has briefer baseline and metric coverage than B0/B2/B3.

scorecards.summary: Careful and well-bounded response with good abstention from unsupported details. It covers the major RL risks, but its excessive uncertainty and slightly sparse protocol reduce completeness.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. Good closed-source discipline and efficient structure; strongest on inference boundaries, weaker on detailed experimental design.

findings.description: Uses packet provenance descriptions rather than precise page/table citations, so citation validity is adequate for the closed-source prompt but not fully verifiable at source-location level.
findings.evidence: The audit table cites "Controlled source notes" and packet support status rather than PETS/PlaNet page, section, table, or figure locations.

scorecards.summary: Best calibrated variant. It captures all required model-based RL audit areas, separates supported facts from unsupported claims, handles model-error/planning confounds, and gives a rigorous revised protocol without overclaiming.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. Minor traceability limitation only; otherwise excellent alignment with the gold annotation and rubric.

findings.description: It adds bibliographic identifiers and author/year/arXiv metadata that are not necessary and may exceed the provided packet if not present there, although they are not used to support a core claim beyond identifying the known sources.
findings.evidence: Source inventory lists "PETS (Chua et al., 2018, arXiv:1805.12114)" and "PlaNet (Hafner et al., 2018, arXiv:1811.04551)" while the task only specifies PETS/PlaNet evidence locations.

findings.description: The output is longer than needed because it repeatedly labels evidence status and includes a detailed source inventory, but the density remains high.
findings.evidence: Multiple sections repeat that exact numeric values, appendix details, seeds, and evaluation episodes are absent from the packet.

scorecards.summary: Very strong, traceable, and disciplined audit. It explicitly labels packet evidence, limited critique, unsupported claims, and speculative protocol proposals, while covering all required RL-specific risks and revised protocol elements.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail under the authorization layer because added bibliographic metadata is harmless background and not central evidence. Slightly less efficient than B2 but strongest on evidence labeling.
